call.output:
1â†’# HW01 - Shapes & GUI: Union Design Pattern
     2â†’
     3â†’## Design Pattern
     4â†’
     5â†’This assignment introduces the **Union Design Pattern**, which models a union of
     6â†’types through an abstract superclass. The key idea is that abstract classes define
     7â†’the invariant behaviors shared by all concrete subclasses, while each subclass
     8â†’provides its own variant implementation. Here, all shapes share a location (the
     9â†’top-left corner of their bounding rectangle), but differ in how they are painted
    10â†’and how they handle color.
    11â†’
    12â†’## Architecture Overview
    13â†’
    14â†’The shape hierarchy follows a classic Union pattern:
    15â†’
    16â†’```
    17â†’AShape (abstract)
    18â†’â”śâ”€â”€ ASimpleShape (abstract) - has its own Color field
    19â†’â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ Rectangle (concrete) - draws a filled rectangle
    20â†’â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ Ellipse (concrete) - draws a filled ellipse
    21â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ Circle (concrete) - extends Ellipse with equal width/height
    22â†’â””â”€â”€ CompositeShape (concrete) - holds two AShape children, delegates to them
    23â†’```
    24â†’
    25â†’A simple Swing GUI (`simpleGUIFrame`) displays shapes in a center panel with
    26â†’buttons that swap which shape is drawn. The "Pimp my GUI" button demonstrates
    27â†’`CompositeShape` by building a car from rectangles and circles.
    28â†’
    29â†’## What Is Provided
    30â†’
    31â†’- **`AShape.java`** (`src/hw01/shape/AShape.java`): Fully implemented abstract
    32â†’  base class with `xPos`/`yPos` fields, getters/setters, and abstract `setColor()`
    33â†’  and `paint()` methods.
    34â†’- **`Circle.java`** (`src/hw01/shape/Circle.java`): Fully implemented. Extends
    35â†’  `Ellipse` with a constructor that passes `diameter` for both width and height.
    36â†’- **`simpleGUIFrame.java`** (`src/hw01/view/simpleGUIFrame.java`): Fully
    37â†’  implemented Swing GUI with buttons for Rectangle, Ellipse, Circle, and a
    38â†’  composite "car" shape. The `paintComponent` override calls `aShape.paint(g)`.
    39â†’
    40â†’## What You Implement
    41â†’
    42â†’### 1. `ASimpleShape.setColor(Color color)` -- `src/hw01/shape/ASimpleShape.java`
    43â†’
    44â†’Set the `color` field (declared as `protected Color color`) to the given color.
    45â†’
    46â†’```java
    47â†’public void setColor(Color color) {
    48â†’    this.color = color;
    49â†’}
    50â†’```
    51â†’
    52â†’### 2. `Rectangle.paint(Graphics g)` -- `src/hw01/shape/Rectangle.java`
    53â†’
    54â†’Paint a filled rectangle using the `Graphics` object. Must:
    55â†’- Set the graphics color to `this.color`
    56â†’- Call `g.fillRect(getXPos(), getYPos(), width, height)`
    57â†’
    58â†’### 3. `Ellipse.paint(Graphics g)` -- `src/hw01/shape/Ellipse.java`
    59â†’
    60â†’Paint a filled ellipse using the `Graphics` object. Must:
    61â†’- Set the graphics color to `this.color`
    62â†’- Call `g.fillOval(getXPos(), getYPos(), width, height)`
    63â†’
    64â†’### 4. `CompositeShape.setColor(Color color)` -- `src/hw01/shape/CompositeShape.java`
    65â†’
    66â†’Delegate `setColor()` to both child shapes (`shape1` and `shape2`).
    67â†’
    68â†’### 5. `CompositeShape.paint(Graphics g)` -- `src/hw01/shape/CompositeShape.java`
    69â†’
    70â†’Delegate `paint()` to both child shapes (`shape1` and `shape2`).
    71â†’
    72â†’## File Paths (Editable)
    73â†’
    74â†’| File | Package | What to implement |
    75â†’|------|---------|-------------------|
    76â†’| `src/hw01/shape/ASimpleShape.java` | `hw01.shape` | `setColor()` |
    77â†’| `src/hw01/shape/Rectangle.java` | `hw01.shape` | `paint()` |
    78â†’| `src/hw01/shape/Ellipse.java` | `hw01.shape` | `paint()` |
    79â†’| `src/hw01/shape/CompositeShape.java` | `hw01.shape` | `setColor()`, `paint()` |
    80â†’
    81â†’## Expected Behavior
    82â†’
    83â†’- **Rectangle**: When painted, a filled rectangle appears at `(getXPos(), getYPos())`
    84â†’  with the specified width, height, and color.
    85â†’- **Ellipse**: When painted, a filled ellipse appears bounded by the rectangle at
    86â†’  `(getXPos(), getYPos())` with the specified width, height, and color.
    87â†’- **Circle**: Inherits from Ellipse; appears as a circle (equal width and height).
    88â†’- **CompositeShape**: When painted, both child shapes paint themselves. When
    89â†’  `setColor()` is called, both children receive the new color. The composite's
    90â†’  position is the minimum x and y of its children (already set in the constructor).
    91â†’- **GUI**: Clicking shape buttons creates shapes and repaints. The "Pimp my GUI"
    92â†’  button creates a composite car shape (rectangle body + two circle wheels).
    93â†’
    94â†’## Testing
    95â†’
    96â†’Run the autograder to validate your implementation:
    97â†’
    98â†’```
    99â†’Use the `grade` tool to run tests.
   100â†’```
   101â†’
   102â†’The test suite (`comp310.tests.hw01.ShapeTest`) verifies:
   103â†’- Shape constructors set position, dimensions, and color correctly
   104â†’- `paint()` methods invoke the correct `Graphics` calls
   105â†’- `CompositeShape` properly delegates `setColor()` and `paint()` to children
   106â†’- The shape hierarchy relationships are correct
   107â†’

1â†’package hw01.shape;
     2â†’
     3â†’import java.awt.Color;
     4â†’import java.awt.Graphics;
     5â†’
     6â†’/**
     7â†’ * 
     8â†’ * @author charlielockyer
     9â†’ * An abstract class describing an abstract simple shape on a 2D plane, like a screen
    10â†’ * A simple shape has its own custom painting instructions, and is not composed of other shapes
    11â†’ * Simple shapes are the most basic shapes and inherently have a color
    12â†’ */
    13â†’public abstract class ASimpleShape extends AShape {
    14â†’	/**
    15â†’	 * The color of the simple shape in the RGB color space; index 0 is R, 1 is G, and 2 is B
    16â†’	 */
    17â†’	protected Color color;
    18â†’
    19â†’	/**
    20â†’	 * Sets the color of the shape as written in AShape
    21â†’	 */
    22â†’	public void setColor(Color color) {
    23â†’		// TODO: Implement this method
    24â†’	}
    25â†’
    26â†’	/**
    27â†’	 * Paints the shape on the screen, abstract because each different shape is painted differently
    28â†’	 */
    29â†’	public abstract void paint(Graphics g);
    30â†’}
    31â†’

1â†’package hw01.shape;
     2â†’
     3â†’import java.awt.Color;
     4â†’import java.awt.Graphics;
     5â†’
     6â†’/**
     7â†’ * 
     8â†’ * @author charlielockyer
     9â†’ * An concrete class describing a composite shape on a 2D plane, like a screen
    10â†’ * A composite shape is composed of two other shapes and delegates its painting instructions to
    11â†’ * those shapes.
    12â†’ * Composite shapes can be composed to an arbitrary degree and do not inherently have color
    13â†’ */
    14â†’public class CompositeShape extends AShape {
    15â†’
    16â†’	/**
    17â†’	 * One component shape of the CompositeShape
    18â†’	 */
    19â†’	private AShape shape1;
    20â†’
    21â†’	/**
    22â†’	 * The other component shape of the CompositeShape
    23â†’	 */
    24â†’	private AShape shape2;
    25â†’
    26â†’	/**
    27â†’	 * Constructs a CompositeShape object
    28â†’	 * @param shape1 the first component shape of the CompositeShape
    29â†’	 * @param shape2 the other component shape of the CompositeShape
    30â†’	 */
    31â†’	public CompositeShape(AShape shape1, AShape shape2) {
    32â†’		this.shape1 = shape1;
    33â†’		this.shape2 = shape2;
    34â†’		this.setXPos(Math.min(shape1.getXPos(), shape2.getXPos()));
    35â†’		this.setYPos(Math.min(shape1.getYPos(), shape2.getYPos()));
    36â†’	}
    37â†’
    38â†’	/**
    39â†’	 * Sets the color of the shape to an input color defined by the Color class by delegating
    40â†’	 * to the component shapes
    41â†’	 * @param color the color of the shape
    42â†’	 */
    43â†’	public void setColor(Color color) {
    44â†’		// TODO: Implement this method
    45â†’	}
    46â†’
    47â†’	/**
    48â†’	 * Paints the shape on the screen, abstract because each different shape is painted differently
    49â†’	 * @param g the graphics object on which the shape will be painted
    50â†’	 */
    51â†’	public void paint(Graphics g) {
    52â†’		// TODO: Implement this method
    53â†’	}
    54â†’}
    55â†’
